messages.author: Jordan
messages.text: Hi from northern Indiana. I have a couple XIAO nRF52840 & Wio-SX1262 Kits I've been playing around with meshtastic firmware. I want to get some real antennas and also be able to flash meshcore. Would I be able to have a lot of fun with these Gizont antennas? https://www.aliexpress.us/item/3256804421300249.html
messages.content_html: Hi from northern Indiana. I have a couple XIAO nRF52840 & Wio-SX1262 Kits I've been playing around with meshtastic firmware. I want to get some real antennas and also be able to flash meshcore. Would I be able to have a lot of fun with these Gizont antennas? https://www.aliexpress.us/item/3256804421300249.html

messages.author: chimeforest
messages.text: I don't personally know the answer.. and this channel doesn't have a lot of people(yet). If you don't get an answer here, you might have better luck if you posted this question in #hardware , #other-meshes , or #node-builds-antennas where there are more people. I'm still new to the server (and meshcore/tastic), so I'm not sure which would be best. (edited)
messages.content_html: I don't personally know the answer.. and this channel doesn't have a lot of people(yet). If you don't get an answer here, you might have better luck if you posted this question in #hardware, #other-meshes, or #node-builds-antennas where there are more people. I'm still new to the server (and meshcore/tastic), so I'm not sure which would be best. (edited)

messages.author: chimeforest
messages.text: Also.. there are adjustable/telescopic antennas on amazon(and I'm sure aliexpress) which you can tune to different frequencies, one of those might work? I have one for my BF-F8HP, but I don't think it has the right connector for meshcore/tastic. (edited)
messages.content_html: Also.. there are adjustable/telescopic antennas on amazon(and I'm sure aliexpress) which you can tune to different frequencies, one of those might work? I have one for my BF-F8HP, but I don't think it has the right connector for meshcore/tastic. (edited)

messages.author: chimeforest
messages.text: The one I have has a little diagram on the base which shows how far to extend it for different frequencies. I don't have it on me rn so don't quote this, but it shows like.. fully closed for 910, 2 segments extended for 850, 1.5 segments for 875, etc etc. I honestly don't use the BF-F8HP very much, so I'd have to go digging to find it for more details. If you got one without such a diagram, it probably wouldn't be too hard to make one yourself so you wouldn't need to bring a ruler with you. (edited)
messages.content_html: The one I have has a little diagram on the base which shows how far to extend it for different frequencies. I don't have it on me rn so don't quote this, but it shows like.. fully closed for 910, 2 segments extended for 850, 1.5 segments for 875, etc etc. I honestly don't use the BF-F8HP very much, so I'd have to go digging to find it for more details. If you got one without such a diagram, it probably wouldn't be too hard to make one yourself so you wouldn't need to bring a ruler with you. (edited)
messages.reply_html:
Jordan
Oh that's interesting! Do you carry a ruler with it?

messages.author: chimeforest
messages.text: Here's what the diagram looks like(found a similar antenna on amz). Each telescoping segment is about 10cm, so if I needed 120mhz, I'd extend it about 6 segments, and if I needed 144mhz I'd extend it about 5 segments, ect. Def not frequencies for LoRa, but hopefully this gives you some ideas
messages.content_html: Here's what the diagram looks like(found a similar antenna on amz). Each telescoping segment is about 10cm, so if I needed 120mhz, I'd extend it about 6 segments, and if I needed 144mhz I'd extend it about 5 segments, ect. Def not frequencies for LoRa, but hopefully this gives you some ideas

messages.author: Jordan
messages.text: @chimeforest very cool! I asked about the different frequencies in #node-builds-antennas , and all the lora meshes are close enough, 906-912 Mhz, that one antenna works for all. That was my guess but I wasn't sure.
messages.content_html: @chimeforest very cool! I asked about the different frequencies in #node-builds-antennas , and all the lora meshes are close enough, 906-912 Mhz, that one antenna works for all. That was my guess but I wasn't sure.

messages.author: chimeforest
messages.text: Excellent, that's good news
messages.content_html: Excellent, that's good news

messages.author: Paul@KC9SEB
messages.text: The default freq for MT == 906.875 if you take the LongFast preset, MC =910.525Mhz.....
messages.content_html: The default freq for MT == 906.875 if you take the LongFast preset, MC =910.525Mhz.....
messages.reply_html:
Jordan
Main question is that meshtastic people like to get them tuned to 907 Mhz. It looks like Meshcore is 910 Mhz or sometimes 908 Mhz in Texas. Would tuning the antenna to 908 Mhz work well enough for all of them?

messages.author: knowmercy
messages.text: Hello, folks. I've been rolling out some repeaters here in northeast ohio. We currently have 4 on the air. Some are not on the map yet, and others are on the map but not deployed entirely just yet. I've got a 45' mast for one but I'm trying to get access from the property owner in some strategic "high elevation" places so I need less nodes.
messages.content_html: Hello, folks. I've been rolling out some repeaters here in northeast ohio. We currently have 4 on the air. Some are not on the map yet, and others are on the map but not deployed entirely just yet. I've got a 45' mast for one but I'm trying to get access from the property owner in some strategic "high elevation" places so I need less nodes.
